codeReview.summary: The feature successfully gives both auth pages a premium gradient/card treatment and uses the shared Button, but it does not fully align with the mission's token conventions and its verification handoff overstates test coverage by citing a RegisterPage test file that was never added.

issues.description: The polished login page still uses hard-coded utility colors such as text-gray-900, text-gray-600, text-gray-700, and bg-white instead of the semantic design-token classes required by the mission's Tailwind token conventions for visual-layer work.

The register redesign likewise relies on hard-coded colors (for example bg-white and text-gray-* classes) instead of the shared semantic tokens, so the page is not fully aligned with the design-system conventions the feature was expected to follow.

The handoff claims login/register coverage and references a register page verification scope, but only __tests__/components/LoginPage.test.tsx exists in the repo and no RegisterPage component test file was added. This makes the reported verification evidence incomplete for the pages changed by the feature.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The ui-worker procedure says to use semantic Tailwind tokens, but this feature still landed with text-gray-* and bg-white classes on the redesigned auth pages. The skill may need a stronger review checklist or explicit grep step for token compliance on polished pages.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: app/login/page.tsx:78-145 and app/register/page.tsx:99-166 contain text-gray-900/text-gray-700/text-gray-600 and bg-white classes despite AGENTS.md saying to always use semantic token classes.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The ui-worker handoff example emphasizes accurately reporting tests, but this worker's handoff overstates coverage by implying broader auth-page verification than exists in the repo. A checklist item to confirm every mentioned test file actually exists would help.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff file 2026-03-26T02-03-44-951Z__login-register-visual-polish__ca969435-5c0b-4e43-8c2d-f300693cf88c.json reports updated tests, while only __tests__/components/LoginPage.test.tsx exists and no RegisterPage test file is present.

summary: Review failed with non-blocking issues: the auth-page polish is visually implemented, but both pages still use hard-coded gray/white classes instead of semantic design tokens, and the handoff overstates test coverage because no RegisterPage component test exists.
